edges.description: Loss of pyrophosphate-mediated mineralization inhibition permits arterial calcium deposition and narrowing.
edges.source: ENPP1/ABCC6 disruption of extracellular pyrophosphate anti-mineralization

edges.description: Intimal proliferation can narrow vessels and compound stenosis from mineral deposition.
edges.source: Reduced AMP/adenosine signaling and myointimal proliferation

nodes.description: Ectopic arterial mineralization and associated stenosis increase vascular resistance, driving severe hypertension, cardiac dysfunction, and high early mortality in severe infantile presentations.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Molecular Characterization of a Patient with Generalized Arterial Calcification of Infancy Caused by Rare ABCC6 Mutation.
evidence.snippet: Generalized arterial calcification of infancy (GACI) is a rare autosomal-recessive disease characterized by extensive arterial calcification in infancy, with clinical manifestations such as arterial stenoses and heart failure.

evidence.reference_title: Preterm Infant with Generalized Arterial Calcification of Infancy Who Survived Due to Early Diagnosis and Appropriate Treatment with Bisphosphonates: A Case Report.
evidence.snippet: GACI causes severe hypertension and heart failure, and approximately 50% of patients die within the first 6 months.

nodes.description: ENPP1 or ABCC6 loss of function perturbs extracellular pyrophosphate and phosphate balance, reducing anti-mineralization capacity and promoting hydroxyapatite deposition in arteries and soft tissues.
nodes.id: ENPP1/ABCC6 disruption of extracellular pyrophosphate anti-mineralization

evidence.snippet: Loss-of-function pathogenic variants of ENPP1 and ABCC6 lead to perturbations in the PPi/Pi ratio, thereby promoting hydroxyapatite mineralization in peripheral tissues.

evidence.reference_title: Prospective phenotyping of long-term survivors of generalized arterial calcification of infancy (GACI).
evidence.snippet: In 67% of cases, GACI is caused by biallelic inactivating variants in ENPP1 (ectonucleotide pyrophosphatase/phosphodiesterase 1),3 which encodes an enzyme that cleaves ATP into AMP and inorganic pyrophosphate (PPi) at the cell surface.

evidence.reference_title: Prospective phenotyping of long-term survivors of generalized arterial calcification of infancy (GACI).
evidence.snippet: While the exact molecule transported by ABCC6 into the extracellular space is uncertain, one candidate is ATP, a substrate for ENPP1 and thus a source of plasma PPi.

nodes.description: ENPP1 deficiency can reduce AMP and downstream adenosine signaling in addition to reducing PPi. This mechanism is proposed to contribute to intimal proliferation, multivessel narrowing, and stenosis even when overt calcification is less prominent.
nodes.id: Reduced AMP/adenosine signaling and myointimal proliferation

evidence.reference_title: Prospective phenotyping of long-term survivors of generalized arterial calcification of infancy (GACI).
evidence.snippet: Specifically, intimal proliferation results from AMP or adenosine deficiency40 and comprises part of the ENPP1-GACI phenotype.
